reasoning: The Federal Funds Rate target range currently has a lower limit of 4.25%. Rate decisions are made at scheduled FOMC meetings, and the next meeting is scheduled to conclude on September 17, 2025. Therefore, there is virtually zero chance of a rate change prior to the first two resolution dates (August 24 and September 16). Inter-meeting rate moves are extremely rare and historically happen almost exclusively to cut rates during crises, not hike them. Looking further out, the Fed has been on an easing/holding cycle, making rate hikes highly unlikely in the near term. For the later resolution dates (November 2025 and February 2026), uncertainty naturally widens, but the overwhelming consensus and base rate points away from rate hikes, keeping probabilities low.
